verdict_reasoning: HSPA1A is predominantly a cytosolic chaperone, but under stress, malignant transformation, and apoptosis it is well-documented to relocalize to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane on tumor cells (via PS/lipid binding and other mechanisms), where it is accessible to antibodies and has been exploited therapeutically (e.g., cmHsp70.1). Surface display is cell-state-induced rather than baseline.
